title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent protocol team receives different fragmentation, peer, trust, timing, and failure constraints and transfers upgrade, connect, tunnels, websocket boundaries, and safe transitions into a new confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.
Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.
Transfer connection, buffered unread octets, deadlines, close ownership, and cancellation atomically to the selected protocol or tunnel.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Upgrade Handshake
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab protocol team handles case 3915 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Upgrade Handshake.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate connection upgrade, upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Upgrade Handshake, keep Upgrade Handshake connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Transition Rejection Recovery
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab protocol team handles case 3923 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Transition Rejection Recovery.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Transition Rejection Recovery
